instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `with-shadowed-symbols` that saves the current values of a list of special variables to gensyms, runs body, and restores the originals via unwind-protect. Body is responsible for any mutation.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro with-shadowed-symbols ((&rest syms) &body body)
  (let ((saved (mapcar (lambda (s) (declare (ignore s)) (gensym)) syms)))
    `(let ,(mapcar (lambda (g s) (list g s)) saved syms)
       (unwind-protect (progn ,@body)
         ,@(mapcar (lambda (g s) `(setf ,s ,g)) saved syms)))))
